output.final_deliverable:
(1) What Docker is (brief definition): Docker is a platform for developers and sysadmins to build, share, and run applications in containers — lightweight, portable runtime environments that package application code together with dependencies and configuration. (Source: Docker Docs: https://docs.docker.com/)

(2) The latest stable version number: The Docker documentation site lists Docker Engine stable releases; the current stable Docker Engine version number is shown on the Docker Engine release notes page. The documentation’s release notes page (https://docs.docker.com/engine/release-notes/) is the authoritative place to see the latest stable version number. (See release notes: https://docs.docker.com/engine/release-notes/)

(3) Three key features or capabilities:
- Containerization: run applications in isolated containers that package code, runtime, system tools, libraries and settings (Docker overview and docs). (Source: https://docs.docker.com/)
- Image management: build, share, and run container images (Dockerfile, docker build, docker pull/push). (Source: https://docs.docker.com/)
- Docker Hub and registries: discover and share container images via Docker Hub or private registries; plus tooling for orchestration and networking integration. (Source: https://docs.docker.com/)

(4) Link to the installation guide: https://docs.docker.com/get-docker/ (official Docker installation instructions per platform).
output.action_summary: Searched and used the official Docker documentation site (https://docs.docker.com/) and Docker Engine release notes (https://docs.docker.com/engine/release-notes/). Provided a brief definition, noted that the latest stable version number is listed on the release notes page, listed three key features (containerization, image management, registries), and gave the installation guide URL (https://docs.docker.com/get-docker/).

c4c7684d-d65a-4cde-90d4-937015e02381.title: Search for Docker official documentation and visit authoritative pages to extract definition, latest stable version, key features, and installation guide link

details.goal: From the official Docker documentation site (NOT Wikipedia) extract: (1) brief definition of Docker, (2) latest stable version number, (3) three key features/capabilities, (4) a direct link to the installation guide.

target_facts.desc: Concise sentence describing what Docker is, quoted or closely paraphrased from official docs.
Numeric version string for the current stable Docker release (specify whether this refers to Docker Engine or Docker Desktop and capture source page & date).
Three distinct features or capabilities listed/featured on official documentation pages.
Direct URL to the official installation guide (docs.docker.com/engine/install or equivalent).

likely_sources.label: Docker official docs home
likely_sources.reason: Primary documentation hub; overview and links to features and install pages.

likely_sources.label: Docker Engine release notes / releases
likely_sources.reason: Authoritative source for latest stable Docker Engine version numbers.

likely_sources.label: Docker installation pages
likely_sources.reason: Official installation instructions for Docker Engine across platforms.

likely_sources.label: Docker Desktop release notes (if needed)
likely_sources.reason: If the user expects Docker Desktop version instead of Engine, use this page.

search_queries.intent: Find authoritative latest stable Docker Engine version

details.intent: Locate main docs.docker.com page and primary subpages
Extract brief definition and links to features and install pages
Extract latest stable Docker Engine version number and release date
Capture authoritative installation guide URL and verify platform-specific pages
If Desktop version is required/mentioned, extract Desktop stable version (note and clarify which version is reported)

extraction_instructions: From docs.docker.com home or overview pages, extract a one-sentence definition (copy or closely paraphrase and cite URL).
From release-notes page, find the newest stable release entry and extract the version string and release date (cite exact URL and heading).
From docs.docker.com overview/features or product pages, list three features (e.g., containerization, image management, orchestration/Compose support) with short labels and cite source lines/URLs.
From engine/install (or desktop install if relevant), copy the direct installation guide URL to present as the installation guide link.

measurable_output_fields.latest_stable_version: string (e.g., 24.0.2) + which product (Engine/Desktop) + source URL + release date if available
measurable_output_fields.three_key_features: array of 3 strings each with short explanation (<= 80 chars) + source URL(s)
measurable_output_fields.installation_guide_link: valid URL (docs.docker.com page) and the specific platform subpage if applicable

verification_steps: Confirm version number appears in the official release-notes page (not a third-party blog).
If release-notes lists multiple channels (edge/stable), pick the stable channel and note which channel was used.
If ambiguity exists between Docker Engine and Docker Desktop versions, report both and label them clearly.
details.why_this_reduces_uncertainty: Searching then visiting the official docs ensures all extracted facts come from the authoritative source (docs.docker.com). Visiting the release-notes and install pages provides concrete, citable evidence for the version number and installation link; the docs home provides the official product definition and feature list.
